preamble: The raising of a low nasalized vowel [ã] to a high nasalized vowel [ĩ] is a less common shift, typically occurring in specific dialectal contexts or as a stage in nasal harmony systems.

examples.note: Observed in some nasal harmony systems where vowels are raised in the presence of high nasal consonants or other high nasalized vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
